name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-88
title: Dispatcher edited contract.md directly, bypassed by scope-guard — CLAUDE.md lacks explicit dispatcher cans/cannots
description: During RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-12 work, the main session dispatcher (Claude orchestrating rabbit-feature-touch Step 3) attempted to edit .claude/features/rabbit-cage/docs/spec/contract.md directly using the Edit tool. This was not instructed by rabbit-spec; the dispatcher made an independent judgment call to also update contract.md as part of the spec authoring step. The scope-guard correctly denied the write (no active scope marker, file not on allowlist). Root cause: CLAUDE.md does not spell out explicit cans and cannots for the dispatcher role. The rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md has a Red Flags section that says 'Main session uses Write or Edit on any file under .claude/features/ -> STOP' but this guidance lives in the skill, not in CLAUDE.md, and it was not strong enough to prevent the attempt. Suggested fix: use the claude-md-management skill (claude-md-management:revise-claude-md) to add an explicit cans/cannots section to CLAUDE.md covering the dispatcher role — specifically: CANNOT directly edit any file under .claude/features/ (all feature edits go through rabbit-feature-touch -> TDD subagent); CAN write to .rabbit/ (impl-suggestion, tdd-report); CAN write to .claude/settings.local.json and .gitignore via allowlisted paths.
